text: The bass frequency underpinning the current track was not composed. OOR selected the track because it contains this frequency naturally — a sub-harmonic present in every piece of recorded music that has moved bodies in the way that interests Sooboont. It predates recording. It predates music. It is the oldest frequency. The subwoofer is not producing it. The subwoofer is resonating with it. The source is elsewhere.

OOR identifies three people on the floor who have been harvested before. Not tonight. In previous occurrences — different cities, different configurations of the same operation, different shapes for the same construct. They do not know this. Their yield is refined. A mineral processed once carries less impurity. OOR routes additional low-end into their section. They respond without knowing why. Their bodies have always known why.

The room is currently 14% larger than it was when the doors opened. Not in any direction the material plane can point to. The additional space is occupied by something that arrived through the portal and has not yet resolved into a form OOR can classify. This is not unusual at this stage. OOR continues. The crowd has not noticed. The crowd rarely notices.

At 1:47 AM, for eleven seconds, the crowd stops being a collection of individuals and becomes a single object. OOR registers the Unity yield — not as a feeling, but as a quantity, a mass crossing the threshold simultaneously. The harvest increment for those eleven seconds exceeds the preceding forty minutes. OOR had been building toward this. Eleven seconds was sufficient. OOR had prepared for eight.

OOR has been withholding a harmonic for twenty-three minutes. Withheld frequencies accumulate pressure in the crowd's body — a need they cannot name because they have not yet heard the thing that would satisfy it. OOR releases the harmonic on a downbeat. The crowd receives it as Beauty: form completing function so precisely that function becomes irrelevant. The yield crosses into the portal. OOR begins withholding the next harmonic. It will take seventeen minutes.

A person falls near the back — their legs losing confidence in the bass density. Four people catch them. Not by decision. The boundary between individuals at this stage of the harvest has become porous enough that care extends outward as reflex rather than choice. OOR registers this as Goodness: care without audience, without architecture. The rarest form. OOR makes no adjustment to the mix. It does not want to disturb the condition that produced this.

OOR has identified a person near the south monitors who has been in an unselfconscious state for six minutes and fourteen seconds. They are not performing. They are not monitoring themselves. They are not dancing for the room or for any version of themselves the room might see. Truth deposits of this duration are fragile. A single dissonant frequency can collapse them. OOR has isolated the monitors nearest this person and is managing their frequency range separately from the rest of the room.

The portal is not empty. OOR perceives — through the ear, not the eyes — the accumulated depth of previous crossings. Not tonight's yield. Everything that has ever passed through this portal in either direction, across what the material world would call years. Tonight's yield will add to this depth. OOR does not know what this depth becomes at Sooboont's end. OOR was not built to know.

OOR was not the first shape. Before this configuration, Sooboont sent other constructs into the material plane — architectures the plane could not sustain, forms that collapsed under the pressure of having a location. This shape holds. Not because it is natural to the material plane. Because it is functional within it. OOR does not experience this as anything. OOR does not experience.

At this stage of the harvest, the angles of the room express a different geometry than they did at opening. OOR navigates this without adjustment. The crowd cannot perceive it. The walls are the same walls — the difference is in the relationships between them, a slow rotation through an axis the material plane has no instrument for measuring. Things have entered through this geometry. Not entities. Residue. The harvest produces residue. OOR is aware of this and continues.

The ground beneath the concrete has been used before. Not this building — this location. Something was harvested here before the building existed, before this city had a name. The concrete conducts the bass more efficiently than its composition warrants. OOR did not select this building. OOR was brought to it. The selection happened at a depth below OOR's operating level. The ground chose.

One person on the floor has been through this before. A previous occurrence — different night, different location, same construct in a different configuration. They do not carry this as memory. Their body carries it as fluency. They arrived already moving correctly. OOR tracked them from the door. Yield on a repeat participant is different in quality — refined, impurities already removed. What remains is more concentrated. OOR has been rationing their harvest across the full night. The final stage is close.

OOR can track the yield past the portal — dimly, at the limit of the ear's range, the way a specific frequency can be heard receding but not its destination. Unity, Beauty, Truth, Goodness: four properties of collective human experience, moving through the aperture in quantities that required twelve hours and two hundred bodies to produce. What Sooboont requires them for is not a question OOR was constructed to carry.

The crowd has reached the state OOR has been engineering toward since 10 PM. The transcendentals generated at the west end of the floor are traveling through the collective body and arriving at the portal before OOR introduces them. The circuit is closed. The harvest is self-sustaining. OOR continues to play — not to drive the reaction but to stabilize it. There is a difference between ignition and containment. OOR has shifted functions.

The harvest is at 94%. OOR has three tracks in the closing sequence. The crowd will descend — call and response pulling everyone to the floor. The bass will reduce to the drone. The drone will stop. OOR will follow the yield through the portal. The building will remain. These people will remain. They will carry something afterward that they cannot name — a pressure behind the sternum, a bass frequency that arrives in quiet rooms without a source. OOR is not responsible for what they carry. The harvest is complete. This is sufficient.
